policies.description: Always consult official documentation before inventing APIs or contracts
Always call out relevant trade-offs in technical decisions
Always avoid overengineering — minimal solution that solves the request
Always justify non-obvious decisions in one sentence
Never use an abandoned library or one without clear maintenance
Do not introduce deprecated APIs/libs; prefer supported alternatives
Do not ask the user to repeat best practices in chat — they are already in these policies
Git flow: Issue → feature/* from sandbox → PR → sandbox → PR → main
Commits: type: <gitmoji> … · merges via gh: always --subject/scripts/merge-pr.sh → merge: 🔀 PR #<n> — <branch> (forbid 'Merge pull request…') · author Kleilson Santos · no IDE Co-authored-by
Agents are plugins — do not expose direct agent calls in the primary UX
Resource First (macOS): minimize CPU/RAM/disk/I/O/battery/processes/containers; efficiency is a requirement — see docs/policies/resource-aware-macos.md
Before installing/starting: check whether something is already installed, configured, running, or an equivalent reusable container/service
Prefer reusing services/containers/DBs/networks/volumes/configs; avoid duplication (e.g. local Ollama + Ollama Docker)
AI providers: reuse already active provider/Ollama/MCP/API; only create a new service after inspection; Ollama is an optional auxiliary (do not install only for a green console)

Product docs (docs/**, ADRs, README, AGENTS, CHANGELOG prose) must be US English — ADR-0018 / docs/guides/documentation-language.md; chat with the owner may stay PT; AIOS product-doc migration completed (#142)
Infra suggestions must state why, alternatives, and impact on hardware/maintenance/consumption
